skill.name: meeting-insights-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes meeting transcripts to identify communication patterns, meeting behaviors, and actionable coaching opportunities.
skill.summary: Turns meeting transcripts into practical communication feedback.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, weak cryptography terms, and reconnaissance keywords, but review found no executable code, command execution, cryptography, network probing, or prompt injection attempt. The skill is prompt-only and safe to publish with a low privacy warning because it asks the assistant to inspect local meeting transcript files.

low_findings.title: Local Transcript File Access Requires User Care
low_findings.description: The skill asks users to download meeting transcripts to a folder, scan that folder, and analyze transcript contents. This is expected for the skill, but meeting transcripts can contain confidential participant, business, or customer information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly directs transcript collection and folder scanning. This is legitimate functionality, but the privacy risk is real because meeting transcripts often contain sensitive data.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The detected backtick execution findings occur in markdown inline code or fenced prompt examples. No Ruby, shell command invocation, or executable script behavior was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown formatting and sample prompts. There is no executable file, command runner, or user input passed to a shell.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are caused by ordinary meeting-analysis language such as communication, scan the folder, and communication requests. No cryptographic operation, system reconnaissance, or network reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are natural-language descriptions and examples for communication analysis. I found no evidence of hashing, encryption, host enumeration, port scanning, or network access.

content.value_statement: Meeting transcripts contain useful feedback, but patterns are hard to see across long conversations. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find communication habits, examples, and improvement steps.
actual_capabilities: Reviews transcript files for speaker labels, timestamps, and date ranges.
Identifies patterns such as interruptions, hedging, filler words, and speaking ratios.
Finds examples of active listening, direct feedback, and missed facilitation opportunities.
Compares multiple meetings to show trends in communication behavior.
Produces structured summaries with findings, examples, recommendations, and next steps.
limitations: Requires readable transcript files or meeting notes supplied by the user.
Accuracy depends on speaker labels, timestamps, and transcript quality.
Does not verify audio tone or body language unless transcripts include that context.
May need user clarification to identify the correct speaker or analysis goal.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a performance review
use_cases.description: Collect examples of communication strengths, growth areas, and leadership behaviors from recent meetings.

use_cases.title: Improve meeting facilitation
use_cases.description: Find speaking balance, inclusion gaps, action item clarity, and moments where disagreement was handled well or poorly.

use_cases.title: Coach communication habits
use_cases.description: Analyze filler words, indirect language, interruptions, and listening signals to create focused practice goals.

prompt_templates.title: Basic transcript review
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with one folder of meeting transcripts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the meeting transcripts in this folder. Summarize my main communication patterns and include specific examples.

prompt_templates.title: Focused behavior analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Review one communication habit in detail.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these transcripts for moments where I avoided direct feedback. Include timestamps, quotes, impact, and a better approach.

prompt_templates.title: Facilitation assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my team meetings for facilitation style. Assess speaking balance, inclusion, decision clarity, and follow-up quality.

prompt_templates.title: Trend comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my Q1 and Q2 meeting transcripts. Show changes in listening, interruptions, directness, questions, and action item clarity.

output_examples.input: Analyze five team meeting transcripts for communication patterns.
output_examples.output: The analysis identifies high speaking time, frequent clarifying questions, two missed chances to invite quieter participants, and three strong examples of summarizing decisions.

output_examples.input: Find moments where I avoided giving direct feedback.
output_examples.output: The output lists repeated hedging, indirect requests, and topic changes. Each example includes a timestamp, the observed language, the impact, and a clearer alternative.

output_examples.input: Compare this month with last month.
output_examples.output: The comparison shows fewer interruptions, more follow-up questions, and improved action item clarity, with remaining work on direct disagreement.

best_practices: Use clear file names with meeting dates and topics.
Analyze one behavior at a time when you need detailed coaching.
Remove or redact sensitive transcript sections before sharing them with any AI tool.
anti_patterns: Do not analyze transcripts without permission from the meeting participants.
Do not treat transcript analysis as a complete measure of leadership skill.
Do not compare people without considering role, context, and meeting purpose.

faq.question: What transcript formats can this skill use?
faq.answer: It is designed for text-based transcripts such as TXT, Markdown, VTT, SRT, and exported meeting notes.

faq.question: Can it analyze meeting recordings directly?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on transcripts. Recordings need to be transcribed before meaningful analysis.

faq.question: Does it send meeting data to another service?
faq.answer: No network behavior is defined in the skill. Data handling depends on the AI tool and environment you use.

faq.question: Can it identify my speaking ratio?
faq.answer: Yes, if the transcript has reliable speaker labels and enough timing or turn information.

faq.question: Can it help with leadership coaching?
faq.answer: Yes. It can highlight facilitation, directness, listening, inclusion, and follow-up patterns from transcripts.

faq.question: How should I protect sensitive meetings?
faq.answer: Redact confidential names, customer details, and private business information before analysis.
